title: gilia tenuiflora subsp. cana
intro: Gilia tenuiflora subsp. cana, also known as the white gilia or Sierra gilia, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Gilia tenuiflora subsp. cana include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and bronchitis. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve making tea from its flowers and leaves, or using it as a tincture or infusion in traditional medicine.
